analysis:
MARKET SNAPSHOT: Nifty and Sensex are likely to face volatility this week due to escalating US-Iran tensions impacting crude oil prices, as highlighted in the Mint headline "US-Iran war to crude oil prices: Top five triggers that may dictate Indian stock market this week." Key levels to watch are Nifty 21,800 (resistance) and 21,500 (support), while Sensex may see resistance at 72,500 and support at 71,500. Geopolitical risks could push markets lower if crude oil prices spike above $80 per barrel.

STOCKS IN FOCUS:

1. Reliance Industries - Oil prices directly impact its refining margins. If crude rises, expect short-term pressure, but long-term resilience due to integrated operations. (Mint headline on crude oil triggers)
2. ONGC - Higher crude prices benefit upstream oil players. Watch for a potential 3-5% upside if oil sustains above $78. (Mint headline on crude oil triggers)
3. Tata Motors - Auto sector may face margin pressure if fuel prices rise. Monitor for a 2-3% correction if crude spikes. (Mint headline on crude oil triggers)
4. IndiGo (InterGlobe Aviation) - Aviation stocks are highly sensitive to fuel costs. A 5% drop is possible if oil crosses $80. (Mint headline on crude oil triggers)
5. Asian Paints - FMCG and paint sectors may see profit booking if crude rises, as input costs increase. Watch for a 2-4% dip. (Mint headline on crude oil triggers)

SECTOR HEAT:
Green (Hot) Sectors:
Oil & Gas - Upstream players like ONGC and Oil India benefit from higher crude prices. (Mint headline on crude oil triggers)
Defence - Geopolitical tensions could boost defence stocks like Bharat Dynamics and Hindustan Aeronautics. (Mint headline on US-Iran war)

Red (Cold) Sectors:
Aviation - Fuel cost sensitivity makes this sector vulnerable. SpiceJet and IndiGo at risk. (Mint headline on crude oil triggers)
FMCG - Input cost inflation may hurt margins. Stocks like Hindustan Unilever and Britannia could dip. (Mint headline on crude oil triggers)
Auto - Higher fuel prices may dampen demand, impacting Tata Motors and Mahindra & Mahindra. (Mint headline on crude oil triggers)

FII/DII:
Foreign Institutional Investors (FIIs) may adopt a cautious stance due to global risks, potentially reducing inflows into Indian equities. Domestic Institutional Investors (DIIs) could step in to buy selectively, especially in defensive sectors like IT and pharma. (Mint headline on US-Iran war and crude oil triggers suggests risk-off sentiment)

RUPEE:
The Indian rupee may weaken against the US dollar due to rising crude oil prices and risk aversion. Expect the rupee to trade in the 83.20-83.60 range, with downside bias if oil spikes. (Mint headline on crude oil triggers)

sources.title: US-Iran war to crude oil prices: Top five triggers that may dictate Indian stock market this week - Mint
